intended_use: EnsoSleep is intended for use for the diagnostic evaluation by a physician to assess sleep quality and as an aid for physicians in the diagnosis of sleep disorders and respiratory related sleep disord
intended_use_full: EnsoSleep is intended for use for the diagnostic evaluation by a physician to assess sleep quality and as an aid for physicians in the diagnosis of sleep disorders and respiratory related sleep disorders in pediatric and adult patients as follows: Pediatric patients ages 13 years and older with polysomnography (PSG) tests obtained in a Hospital or Sleep Clinic; Adult patients with PSGs obtained in a Hospital or Sleep Clinic; Adult patients with Home Sleep Tests. EnsoSleep is a software-only medical device to be used under the supervision of a clinician to analyze physiological signals and automatically score sleep study results, including the staging of sleep, detection of arousals, leg movements, and sleep disordered breathing events including obstructive apneas (OSA), central sleep apneas (CSA), and hypopneas.
target_condition: Sleep disorders and respiratory-related sleep disorders, including obstructive sleep apnea, central sleep apnea, and other sleep disordered breathing events
ground_truth: 2/3 Majority Scoring consensus from nine registered polysomnography technologists (RPSGTs) with 5-20+ years clinical experience; reference device Michele Sleep Scoring device (K112102) for specific comparison on hypopnea and central sleep apnea events
other_metrics: Positive Agreement (PA), Negative Agreement (NA), Overall Agreement (OA), Kappa, Deming Regression analysis, Bland-Altman mean difference and limits of agreement. For sleep staging: Adult Wake PA 93.5%, N2 PA 88.3%, REM PA 90.9%; Pediatric Wake PA 93.1%, N2 PA 92.6%, N3 PA 92.3%, REM PA 80.9%. For OSA diagnosis: Adult AHI≥5 PA 94.4% NA 89.7%, AHI≥15 PA 94.0% NA 96.3%. For respiratory rate: ≥90% of epochs within ≤2 breaths per minute of reference standard.
